content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 5,
1994
Pamela Morey
Director
Westport County Water District
do Victor Mueller
25620 Ward Avenue
Fort Bragg,
CA 95437-8459
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-94—058
Dear Ms. Morey:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
This letter
shall also serve to confirm our telephone conversation of
January 25,
1994.
Specifically,
you asked if you violated the Act
by voting on agenda items which involved Sandra Bruning.
2
Your request does not address a pending decision but rather
seeks comment regarding conduct of yours that has already taken
place.
We cannot provide you with advice concerning that conduct.
Regulation 18329(b) (8)A)
provides that formal written, advice must
be declined where the “requestor is seeking advice relating to
past conduct.”
However, the Commission can provide advice that is
prospective in nature.
In your letter you also asked if you may
vote on unfair billing practices.
Because your question is
general in nature and your letter does not seek advice regarding a
specific pending governmental decision,
we treat your letter as a
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
In your letter you state that Sandra Bruning is a neighbor and
friend of yours.
You helped her type up county forms and get
through the county process of acquiring a use permit for a bed and
breakfast inn plus a gift shop.
You were listed on her use permit
as her “Agent.”
Ms.
Bruning’s permit was granted by the county
and became final September 14,
1993.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—94-058
Page
2
request for informal assistance pursuant to the provisions of
Regulation 18329.
You implied in your letter that a decision concerning billing
practices will have a financial effect on Sandra Bruning,
owner of
the Blue Victorian Inn,
and Velma Bowen,
owner of the Pelican
Lodge and Inn.
You also stated in your letter that you have not
received any money from Ms.
Bruning for helping her obtain a use
permit for her bed and breakfast inn and gift shop; nor do you
have any financial interest in her business; nor have you received
any gifts from Ms. Bruning in the amount of $250 or more.
The Act prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.
(Section 87100.)
The Act
specifies that an official has a financial interest if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally,
on the official or a member of his or her immediate
family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income,
other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status., aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)


Our File No.
1-94-058
Page
3
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
(Section 87103.)
From the information you have provided, it does not appear
that Sandra Bruning is an economic interest of yours.
4
Thus,
absent some disqualifying economic interest as set forth in
Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on you personally
resulting from a county water board decision concerning billing
practices, you may make, participate in making,
or use your
official position to influence such a decision.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
i,c& ‘ua-’
By:
Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel
This letter is based on the facts presented to us.
The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Ogelsby
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 71.)
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10c per page.

sections.question: Specifically, you asked if you violated the Act by voting on agenda items which involved Sandra Bruning. In your letter you also asked if you may vote on unfair billing practices.
sections.conclusion: From the information you have provided, it does not appear that Sandra Bruning is an economic interest of yours. Thus, absent some disqualifying economic interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on you personally resulting from a county water board decision concerning billing practices, you may make, participate in making, or use your official position to influence such a decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Did the requestor violate the Political Reform Act by voting on agenda items involving Sandra Bruning, and may the requestor vote on unfair billing practices that could affect two inn owners?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The requestor has no disqualifying economic interest in Sandra Bruning and may vote on billing practices decisions that affect the inn owners, provided there is no direct financial effect on the requestor personally.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter. The QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are clearly present in the body text. Note: The requestor's prior conduct regarding voting on Sandra Bruning matters was declined for advice (past conduct); only the prospective question about voting on billing practices was addressed as informal assistance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Specifically, you asked if you violated the Act by voting on agenda items which involved Sandra Bruning. In your letter you also asked if you may vote on unfair billing practices.

CONCLUSION: From the information you have provided, it does not appear that Sandra Bruning is an economic interest of yours. Thus, absent some disqualifying economic interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on you personally resulting from a county water board decision concerning billing practices, you may make, participate in making, or use your official position to influence such a decision.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to a water district director's request regarding whether she violated conflict-of-interest laws by voting on matters involving Sandra Bruning and unfair billing practices. The Commission concluded that absent a disqualifying economic interest, the requestor may vote on the billing practices decision.
